title: Food Pantries * Homeless Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Food Pantries * Homeless Youth 8 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community AIDS/HIV At Risk Youth Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Families Homeless Youth Mental Health Issues Seasonal Workers Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides basic needs, essential support services, crisis intervention, and advocacy. Services include a drop-in center, food pantry, children's clothing, school supplies, personal grooming supplies, feminine hygiene supplies, mentoring, information and referrals for other assistance agencies, and assistance in applying for identification. 817 S 10th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 and their families who are experiencing homelessness Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 1pm-5pm Summer: Tuesday-Wednesday 1pm-5pm (541) 267-3104 7115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT TEEN DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Offers a food pantry, help applying for state benefits such as OHP and SNAP, showers, laundry facilities. Also offers assistance with educational needs such as re-enrollment, school supplies, and specialized information and referral. Eligibility: Ages 11-24 that are homeless and unaccompanied by an adult Hours: Tuesday/Thursday noon-4pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES ADMIN OFFICE RESOURCE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelf-stable food pantry, basic personal care items (such as toothbrushes and deodorant), and clothing. 1790 W 11th Avenue Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-2688 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RURAL PROGRAM LOOKING GLASS RURAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with access to food, clothing, and other vital resources. Provides case management services to assist youth in finding and maintaining stable housing. Also helps with employment including resume preparation, job skills training, and connections to employment opportunities. 620 E Whiteaker Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Youth ages 11-21 who are homeless, runaways, or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 767-3823 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD RESOURCE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelf-stable food pantry, basic personal care items (such as toothbrushes and deodorant), and clothing. 1403 F Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-2688 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES WEST EUGENE RESOURCE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelf-stable food pantry, basic personal care items (such as toothbrushes and deodorant), and clothing. 260 E 11th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 484-4428 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RESOURCE PANTRY LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES LOOKING GLASS RURAL PROGRAM LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RESOURCE PANTRY LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RESOURCE PANTRY MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD
